channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Can Chinese AI solve inequality? + How dementia comes for your bank account
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: Trump wants to suspend the federal gas tax as prices soar amid war with Iran
item.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.
item.content:encoded:
Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: 'Atlanta Journal-Constitution' chief steps down as bold goals yield to tough realities
item.description: The owners of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution invested $150 million to reinvent the paper. The changes have been significant. Three years in, the payoff has been modest.
item.content:encoded:
The owners of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution invested $150 million to reinvent the paper. The changes have been significant. Three years in, the payoff has been modest.

(Image credit: Paras Griffin)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
item.description: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Oregon's most unexpected gubernatorial candidate? A pencil with a point
item.description: Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.
item.content:encoded:
Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.

(Image credit: Eli Imadali)
item.dc:creator: Dirk VanderHart

item.title: As ranks of uninsured grow, charity care can be hard to come by at many hospitals
item.description: An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.
item.content:encoded:
An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.

(Image credit: Anthony Souffle)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says
item.description: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.

(Image credit: Narges Foundation Archive)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: It's not shameful, it's savvy: The shoppers redefining how to save money on groceries
item.description: Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.
item.content:encoded:
Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Marketers say NAD+ pills and infusions can boost longevity. What's the evidence?
item.description: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.content:encoded: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: How do you cut your food bill? NPR wants to hear your tips
item.description: With rising food costs, NPR wants to hear your tips and hacks for reducing your bill.
item.content:encoded:
With rising food costs, NPR wants to hear your tips and hacks for reducing your bill.

(Image credit: sergeyryzhov)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez
